channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? The view from the semi-finalists
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to journalists and fans from the World Cup's four semi-finalists to see who they think will win the tournament.

item.title: Time for Lamine Yamal to ignite? Or another Mbappe World Cup masterclass?
item.description: France face Spain for a place in the World Cup final on Tuesday, meaning a showdown between Kylian Mbappe and Lamine Yamal. Who comes out on top?

item.title: England to make late call on Rice for World Cup semi-final
item.description: England will make a late decision on whether Declan Rice is fit to start Wednesday's World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

item.title: Increased security for England and Argentina tie
item.description: Authorities say there will be increased security measures in place for Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina in Atlanta.

item.title: 'Easy to create divisions' - Kane says England are 'completely together'
item.description: England captain Harry Kane says the squad are "completely together" before their World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

item.title: Timing took me to Chelsea rather than Liverpool - Alonso
item.description: Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso says his arrival at Stamford Bridge instead of Liverpool was "about timings".

item.title: Man Utd sign £48m Santos and close in on Tielemans
item.description: Manchester United sign Andrey Santos from Chelsea and are in advanced talks with Aston Villa for Youri Tielemans.

item.title: Deschamps predicts 'spectacular' France v Spain semi-final
item.description: France boss Didier Deschamps is predicting a "spectacular" semi-final when his exciting side take on Spain in a heavyweight World Cup showdown in Dallas.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 37
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Dutch referee dies weeks after being dropped from World Cup
item.description: Dutch football referee Rob Dieperink dies aged 38, weeks after he was dropped from officiating at the World Cup because of a police investigation in the UK.

item.title: Chris Sutton's World Cup semi-final predictions
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the semi-final ties at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: All the goals from France's run to semi-finals
item.description: Watch every goal scored by France on their way to the 2026 World Cup semi-finals and a mouth-watering tie against Spain.

item.title: Spain's road to semi-final - every goal so far
item.description: Relive every goal Spain have scored on their way to the 2026 World Cup semi-finals, where a thrilling meeting with France at Dallas Stadium awaits.

item.title: Argentina v England 1998 - Owen, Beckham and penalty pain
item.description: England meet Argentina in the last 16 of the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: 'Written off as too old' - France beat up-and-coming Spain in 2006
item.description: Spain meet France in the last 16 of the 2006 World Cup.

item.title: Argentina v England 2002 - Redemption for Beckham
item.description: England meet Argentina in the group stage of the 2002 World Cup.

item.title: Most clinical? Least creative? Best in the air? Comparing the last four
item.description: BBC Sport compares statistics for France, Spain, Argentina and England in the World Cup so far as the semi-finalists prepare to meet.

item.title: Villa working on deal for Switzerland's Manzambi
item.description: Aston Villa are working on a deal for Freiburg midfielder Johan Manzambi as they attempt to hijack a move to Newcastle United.

item.title: Villa assess Estupinan move - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Aston Villa assess Pervis Estupinan, Bruno Guimaraes is not for sale, Inter Milan give up on signing Trevoh Chalobah, plus more

item.title: Alonso wants to keep Fernandez at Chelsea
item.description: New Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso is unveiled at Stamford Bridge and says he wants to keep Enzo Fernandez at the club.

item.title: BBC experts pick their England World Cup semi-final XI - do you agree?
item.description: England are through to the World Cup semi-finals, but who would you pick in your starting XI?

item.title: I want to give fans a Liverpool team to be proud of - Iraola
item.description: Andoni Iraola wants to give Liverpool fans "a team that they can be proud of" and says he is ready for the responsibility of such a big job.

item.title: Borrowed shoes, no Golden Boot - the story of the World Cup's greatest scorer
item.description: Just Fontaine's record of 13 goals in a single World Cup has stood for decades, but he never played in the tournament again after injury ended his career at the age of 28.

item.title: These are unprecedented times - England should enjoy them
item.description: A record of four semi-finals in the past five international tournaments for England is outstanding. It is time to accept that this is where England belong.

item.title: Argentina to wear 'lucky' blue away kit against England
item.description: Argentina will wear their navy blue away strip and England their all-white home strip when the two sides face each other in the World Cup semi-finals on Wednesday.

item.title: Which Premier League clubs have been hit hardest by World Cup?
item.description: Which Premier League clubs have carried the biggest World Cup workload?

item.title: How valuable are Engels, Maeda & McGregor to Celtic?
item.description: Martin O'Neill has spoken of his doubts over the futures at Celtic of Arne Engels, Daizen Maeda and Callum McGregor, but what would their departures mean for the Scottish champions?

item.title: Ted Lasso actor Fernandez makes professional debut
item.description: Ted Lasso actor Cristo Fernandez makes his professional football debut for American second-tier side El Paso Locomotive.

item.title: Liverpool sign England goalkeeper Keating
item.description: Liverpool sign England goalkeeper Khiara Keating on a free transfer from Manchester City.

item.title: How Maradona dominated England in 1986
item.description: Diego Maradona leads his Argentina team to victory against England in the quarter-final of the World Cup in 1986.

item.title: All the goals from England's 2026 World Cup campaign so far
item.description: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there.

item.title: Bellingham scores extra-time winner as England reach semi-finals
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores twice, including an extra-time winner, as England come from behind to beat Norway 2-1 in Miami to reach the World Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Argentina set up England semi-final with extra-time victory over Switzerland
item.description: Argentina set up a World Cup semi-final with England after scoring twice in extra-time to beat 10-man Switzerland 3-1 in Kansas City.

item.title: Was VAR right to overturn England penalty?
item.description: England have a penalty overturned following a VAR review after Djed Spence was judged to have initiated the contact with Norway's Oscar Bobb during the first half of extra time in Miami.

item.title: Did spider cam impact England's opener?
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney, Ellen White and Micah Richards look at whether the spider cam had an impact in Jude Bellingham's first goal for England against Norway.

item.title: Alvarez's stunning strike gives Argentina lead against 10-man Switzerland
item.description: Argentina retake the lead in their quarter-final against Switzerland through a stunning strike from Julian Alvarez during extra-time in Kansas City.

item.title: England 'got lucky' with disallowed goal
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney and Ellen White believe England "got lucky" after Torbjorn Heggem's second-half goal was ruled out for a foul on Elliott Anderson by Erling Haaland.

item.title: 'Man of all the moments' - Bellingham gives England the lead
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores his second goal of the game to give England a 2-1 lead over Norway during extra time of their 2026 World Cup quarter-final in Miami.

item.title: England top air miles among semi-finalists - does it matter?
item.description: England have travelled far more than rivals France and Argentina as World Cup 2026 tests teams across North America.

item.title: Bilic reappointed as Croatia manager
item.description: Croatia reappoint former defender Slaven Bilic as the manager of their men's national team.

item.title: Everton captain Finnigan signs one-year extension
item.description: Everton captain Megan Finnigan signs a one-year contract extension with the Women's Super League club.

item.title: Police chief makes plea to England fans in UK
item.description: Police urge England fans at home to follow the example of supporters in the United States after several incidents over the weekend.

item.title: 'Animals', Hand of God and Beckham - Argentina and England's World Cup rivalry
item.description: As England and Argentina prepare to go head-to-head in Wednesday's World Cup semi-final, BBC Sport looks at their footballing rivalry.

item.title: Top three should be Hearts' focus - McGowan
item.description: Hearts' focus should be staying clear in the Scottish Premiership's top three, says former defender Ryan McGowan.

item.title: Spain defender Leon joins London City Lionesses
item.description: Spain defender Mapi Leon joins London City Lionesses on a three-year deal following her exit from Barcelona.

item.title: Aberdeen buy winger Smith from Livingston
item.description: Aberdeen buy Lewis Smith from Livingston for an undisclosed fee, with manager Stephen Robinson admitting he had attempted to sign the winger for previous clubs without success.

item.title: 'We have another level we can reach' - Kane defends frustrated Tuchel
item.description: Captain Harry Kane says England "have another level we can reach" as they bid to win the World Cup for the first time since 1966.

item.title: Infantino opens door to 64-team World Cup
item.description: Plans for a 64-team men's world cup are set to be picked up and assessed in detail after the 2026 tournament with Fifa boss Gianni Infantino saying football needs to be "for the whole world".

item.title: Lamine Yamal? Mbappe? Kane? Pick your World Cup semi-final XI
item.description: As we look ahead to the World Cup semi-final, BBC Sport pick their last-four combined XI. Do you agree?

item.title: BBC to show England's World Cup semi-final
item.description: England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina will be broadcast live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer.

item.title: Bombs, blood, ambushes - football coaching with the army
item.description: From kidnappings to invasions, military training takes football coaches out of their comfort zone as part of the Football Association of Wales' Uefa pro licence course.

item.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
item.description: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

item.title: Tuchel unhappy with England display - so why do they keep winning?
item.description: Football tactics correspondent Umir Irfan examines why England are still winning games despite Thomas Tuchel insisting performances are not good.

item.title: Merino the unlikely hero again - as Spain wait for Lamine Yamal show
item.description: Mikel Merino came off the bench to send Spain into the World Cup semi-finals - but Lamine Yamal is still waiting for his moment to truly shine.

item.title: What's going on at Newcastle with Guimaraes' future in doubt?
item.description: Bruno Guimaraes joined Newcastle in January 2022 and hoped to fulfil all his ambitions at the club. If he leaves to join Arsenal, what does it mean for the Magpies' direction?

item.title: Premier League pre-season - who are your club playing?
item.description: The World Cup is still going on but Premier League clubs will soon begin their pre-season friendlies - who is your club taking on and when?

item.title: A striker duel for the ages & Qatar revenge - what to expect in quarter-finals
item.description: Setting the scene for each of the four intriguing World Cup quarter-finals that begin on Thursday.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the key dates for the 2026-27 football season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains the full list of key dates for the 2026-27 men's football season

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Chermiti claims no plans to leave Rangers - gossip
item.description: Youssef Chermiti says he has no intention of leaving Rangers, but Celtic's Daizen Maeda repeats his ambition to play in England's Premier League as Heart of Midlothian are told one of their targets is not for sale.

item.title: Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?
item.description: Your one-stop shop for all the ins and outs in the Scottish Premiership during the 2026 summer transfer window.

item.title: O'Sullivan set to leave Liverpool six months after joining
item.description: Liverpool club record signing Denise O'Sullivan is expected to leave this summer just six months after joining.

item.title: England defender Charles joins Man City from Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City have signed England international Niamh Charles for £500,000 from Women's Super League rivals Chelsea.

item.title: Arsenal sign Spain defender Batlle on free transfer
item.description: Arsenal sign Spain international Ona Batlle on a free transfer following her exit from Barcelona.

item.title: West Ham sign forward Salmon on two-year deal
item.description: West Ham have signed England forward Ebony Salmon on a two-year deal following her exit from Aston Villa.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.
